Maternal Depression and Anxiety | Shasta County CA
Toggle Menu
Maternal Depression and Anxiety
You Are Not Alone. You Are Not to Blame. You Can Feel Better.
If you’re feeling out of control, irritable, sad, anxious, angry, or depressed — know that you're not alone. These feelings are more common than you might think, and
you are not to blame
. With support, healing is possible. Anxiety and depression are real medical conditions, and they are treatable.
Your mental health matters — not only for your own well-being, but also for the healthy growth and development of your baby. When left untreated, anxiety and depression can make it harder to bond and connect with your child.
Healthy Moms, Healthy Babies Program
The
Healthy Moms, Healthy Babies Program
is here to help. We support pregnant women and moms parenting children under age 2 by connecting them to compassionate care and local resources tailored to their needs. You deserve support — and we’re here to walk with you every step of the way.
The Healthy Moms, Healthy Babies Program Can Help You:
Connect with appropriate treatment resources
Receive tools to strengthen the bond between you and your baby
Access ongoing monthly support from a caring Public Health Nurse
For more information, contact a Public Health Nurse at:
(530) 225-5850 or toll-free at (800) 300-5122
See Healthy Moms, Healthy Babies Program flyer that can be found under the Resource section
Mother-to-Mother Peer Group
The Mother -to- Mother group provides a safe space for mothers who are experiencing anxiety and depression. This group allows mothers to connect with others who may have had similar experience and guides you in learning different ways to manage anxiety and depression.
Classes will start the beginning of January and run until June 2026. Please see the Mother-to-Mother flyer that can be found under the Resource tab.
